meaning: v.目睹,认识,洞悉,辨别,看清楚
examples: The adult observers were able to reliably discern an assortment of emotions on the babies' faces.
In the real world, problems rarely come neatly packaged, so being able to discern their deep structure is key.
We could just discern the house in the distance.
At the same time, I sought to discern the point around which my own transition would pivot.
In any sport, it takes only a glance--two seconds, tops--to discern that sport's particular defining talent.
